definition:
1. Lat. non castigatus, unchastised, undisciplined, unbridled, (Herdotus Historicus), attic, etc. 
2. licentious, intemperate , opposite to σώφρων, (Sophocles Tragicus), etc.:—; so in adverb, ἀκολάστως ἔχειν (Plato Philosophus); comparative, ἀκολαστοτέρως ἔχειν πρός τι to be too intemperate  in a thing, (Xenophon Historicus) (ML)
